title: What Scripture actually says about self-control (it's not willpower)
caption:
Self-control isn't white-knuckling it.

Here's what most men get wrong: they treat discipline as a force-of-will game — grit until you break, fail, shame yourself, repeat. That's not what Scripture's teaching.

Galatians 5 puts self-control in the fruit of the Spirit. It's grown, not manufactured. You can't force-produce what only the Spirit gives. But you can cultivate the root it grows from.

That means walking with God first. It means controlling the input before the craving peaks — the morning wins the battle, not the 11 PM resistance. And it means building the small obediences, because a man who can't keep his word to himself in the small doesn't suddenly have it when the stakes are high.

"Walk by the Spirit, and you will not gratify the desires of the flesh." — Galatians 5:16. The order matters.

Save this and share it with a brother who's been white-knuckling it alone.

#faith #christianmen #selfcontrol #scripture #discipline #godlymen #galatians #christianity
theme: He runs his life — his impulses don't control him.
